post.summary: Handle POST request for TUS Resumable uploads
post.description: Creates a new resumable upload and returns its Location URL; the sign/ variant authorizes via the x-signature header from a presigned token instead of a JWT

options.summary: Handle OPTIONS request for TUS Resumable uploads
options.description: Handle OPTIONS request for TUS Resumable uploads

post.summary: Handle POST request for TUS Resumable uploads
post.description: Creates a new resumable upload and returns its Location URL; the sign/ variant authorizes via the x-signature header from a presigned token instead of a JWT

put.summary: Handle PUT request for TUS Resumable uploads
put.description: Appends a chunk of upload data at the given Upload-Offset to an in-progress resumable upload created by the POST step

patch.summary: Handle PATCH request for TUS Resumable uploads
patch.description: Appends a chunk of upload data at the given Upload-Offset to an in-progress resumable upload, per the standard TUS PATCH semantics

head.summary: Handle HEAD request for TUS Resumable uploads
head.description: Returns the current Upload-Offset and Upload-Length of an in-progress upload without transferring any data, and does not require authorization

delete.summary: Handle DELETE request for TUS Resumable uploads
delete.description: Cancels an in-progress resumable upload and discards any data received so far

options.description: Handle OPTIONS request for TUS Resumable uploads

post.description: Creates a new resumable upload and returns its Location URL; the sign/ variant authorizes via the x-signature header from a presigned token instead of a JWT

patch.summary: Handle PATCH request for TUS Resumable uploads

head.summary: Handle HEAD request for TUS Resumable uploads

post.description: Queues asynchronous deletion of all objects inside the bucket without deleting the bucket itself, which may take up to an hour to complete

message.example: Empty bucket has been queued. Completion may take up to an hour.

head.description: Returns buckets owned by the authenticated user, supporting pagination via limit/offset plus sorting and search filters

post.description: The bucket id defaults to the given name when not provided, and the owner is set from the authenticated caller

get.description: Returns buckets owned by the authenticated user, supporting pagination via limit/offset plus sorting and search filters
Requires the caller to have access to the bucket via RLS policies, unlike the public bucket listing

head.description: Requires the caller to have access to the bucket via RLS policies, unlike the public bucket listing

put.description: Requires at least one of public, file_size_limit or allowed_mime_types in the body, and only the fields provided are changed

delete.description: Fails if the bucket still contains objects, so it must be emptied first
Deletes a single object by its exact key, unlike the bulk-delete endpoint which accepts multiple prefixes

put.summary: Update the object at an existing key
put.description: Always overwrites the object at the given key, in contrast to the create-object endpoint which requires x-upsert to overwrite

post.description: Fails if an object already exists at the given key unless the x-upsert header is set to true

delete.description: Accepts a list of object prefixes to delete in one request, capped by a configurable per-request limit

prefixes.description: At most 1000 objects can be deleted per request.

get.description: Requires a valid auth token and checks bucket/object access via RLS, regardless of whether the bucket is public

head.description: Returns object metadata in headers only, with no body, and requires a valid auth token even for public buckets

post.summary: Generate a presigned url to upload an object
post.description: The returned token is valid for the configured upload signed URL expiration time and must be submitted to the presigned upload endpoint; set x-upsert to allow overwriting an existing object

put.summary: Uploads an object via a presigned URL
put.description: Verifies the token query parameter instead of requiring an authorization header, and does not need the caller to have direct bucket access

post.summary: Generate a presigned url to retrieve an object
post.description: Returns a URL valid for the given expiresIn seconds; optional transform options are baked into the signed token so the download URL applies image transformations without further authorization

get.summary: Retrieve an object via a presigned URL
get.description: Requires no authorization header, relying instead on the signed token query parameter, and streams the object bytes rather than metadata

head.summary: Retrieve an object via a presigned URL
head.description: Requires no authorization header, relying instead on the signed token query parameter, and streams the object bytes rather than metadata

post.summary: Generate presigned urls to retrieve objects
post.description: Batches signed URL generation for multiple paths up to the configured max objects per request, returning a per-path error instead of failing the whole request when a path is inaccessible

error.example: Either the object does not exist or you do not have access to it

post.description: Renames the object within the same bucket, or relocates it to destinationBucket when provided, without re-uploading the file content

post.summary: Search for objects under a prefix
post.description: Uses cursor-based pagination instead of limit/offset, and supports an optional delimiter to group results like folders, unlike the legacy list endpoint

post.summary: Search for objects under a prefix
post.description: Legacy listing endpoint using limit/offset pagination and a required prefix, without the cursor-based pagination or delimiter grouping supported by list-v2

get.description: Returns object metadata as a JSON body rather than headers only, and requires a valid auth token even for public buckets

head.description: Returns object metadata as a JSON body rather than headers only, and requires a valid auth token even for public buckets

post.description: Copies metadata along with the object content by default (override via copyMetadata), can target a different destinationBucket, and requires x-upsert to overwrite an existing destination key

get.summary: Retrieve an object from a public bucket
get.description: Requires no authorization header but errors if the bucket is not marked public, unlike the authenticated retrieval endpoint

delete.summary: Purge cache for entire tenant or tenant transformations
delete.description: Pass transformations=true in the query string to purge only cached image transformation renditions instead of the whole tenant cache

delete.summary: Purge cache for an entire bucket or bucket transformations
delete.description: Pass transformations=true in the query string to purge only cached image transformation renditions for the bucket instead of all of its cached responses

delete.summary: Purge cache for an object or object transformations
delete.description: Pass transformations=true in the query string to purge only cached image transformation renditions of the object instead of its plain cached response

head.description: Checks database connectivity and always responds with HTTP 200, reporting healthy: false in the body rather than an error status when the check fails

get.description: Checks database connectivity and always responds with HTTP 200, reporting healthy: false in the body rather than an error status when the check fails

tags.name: s3
tags.description: S3-compatible protocol. Not enumerated here: each operation is dispatched by query string/header on a handful of shared routes, which OpenAPI cannot express as distinct operations. See src/http/routes/s3/commands for the per-command request/response contract, or use any S3 SDK against this endpoint.
